name: The White House
subject: Unleashing the Potential of the American Economy
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=959541b4eaaed819f80a880e063583429d79b4820738ae57832861b26718979b30620ace924d48554f80c9593c8a45ce2c1ed6f3723f30d6

Last night, the Senate passed its fiscal year 2018 Budget Resolution - an important step in advancing the Trump Administration’s pro-growth and pro-jobs legislative agenda. The President’s tax reform plan will double the standard deduction so that more income is taxed at zero percent - the first $12,000 of income for individuals and $24,000 for married couples will be income tax-free. The plan will also lower individual income tax rates to: 12 percent, 25 percent, and 35 percent. Lower taxes means higher wages for hardworking Americans. Learn more about the Unified Framework for Tax Reform at
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=959541b4eaaed8199af893cb80ec40f9d0ebff29fc761f171520e9f7cbe5c45acd8e4b4002f0ac6f7581b8e00871ed6a5f2d5847f702b835
whitehouse.gov/taxreform.

---------------

Governor of Puerto Rico Visits the White House

On Thursday, Governor Ricardo Rosselló of Puerto Rico visited the White House to meet with President Donald J. Trump and discuss the situation on the ground in the wake of Hurricane Maria. President Trump and Governor Rosselló gave remarks to the press regarding the scale of hurricane damage, federal government assistance, and Puerto Rico’s development goals.

Governor Rosselló described the situation as “catastrophic” with “poignant short term needs,” but spoke hopefully of the opportunity for Puerto Rico’s energy and power technology to flourish after recovery and rebuilding occur.

The President commended the tenacity of Puerto Rico’s population in the face of tremendous difficulty. “The people of Puerto Rico are amazing,” President Trump said. “Just incredible people, the spirit they have, the strength they have, what they've gone through.”

Read more
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=959541b4eaaed819fbf5d06aa888819ee0ed748eaa6ce479e8a242007b5e2a9144baaac3b8147487ee8a75bd86f7e49d4b292efff828ab03
here.

Senate Passage of its Fiscal Year 2018 Budget Resolution
This resolution creates a pathway to unleash the potential of the American economy through tax reform and tax cuts, simplifying the overcomplicated tax code, providing financial relief for families across the country, and making American businesses globally competitive.

Read the President's statement
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=959541b4eaaed819c8613a48ce0b02acc3ef631e9a7ffbef4206b2ddcb49dd3a84286fea8bc3938a3c800b85233106db53411c0dd2009f27
here.

Vice President Speaks on Tax Reform in New York
On Tuesday, in New York, Vice President Mike Pence discussed the Unified Framework for Tax Reform. The Vice President spoke with business owners about cutting taxes across the board for working families, small businesses, and family farms.
"This tax cut is first and foremost designed to help American families struggling too often to make ends meet to have more dollars in their pockets," Vice President Pence said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=959541b4eaaed819e0a3d24bcc50a0101364f70953f76cd1e5000eacb959ae095e74fbedc8672aea7d5c442f01762c56c4397d303ae10527
Watch highlights of Vice President Pence's trip.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and Vice President Mike Pence meet with Puerto Rico Governor Ricardo Rosselló | October 19, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Today, the President received his daily intelligence briefing, followed by a meeting with the Secretary-General of the United Nations.

At the Smithsonian Museum of American History, First Lady Melania Trump was joined by designer Hervé Pierre to present her inauguration gown as the newest addition to the nearly 100-year old First Ladies Collection.

COMING UP

On Saturday and Sunday, the White House will be opening its gardens and grounds to visitors for the Fall Garden Tours. Tickets are available on a first-come, first-served basis. Check
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d44511f91f3f1680f0a6c2eeb47b2ec9f1680e07fc51ff95c8323124578274dcb80d252d4f63c2ee138c26c902174641828db78d1b8d696d
here for more information.

On Monday, President Trump will award the Medal of Honor to United States Army Captain Gary M. Rose (Retired) for conspicuous gallantry during the Vietnam War. The Prime Minister of Singapore will also be visiting the White House on Monday.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d44511f91f3f1680330645c09e6a560ea3c9f2772d7cc7f5abd94b2d46e774176d980afe1d9b8c71a8935e26e4f020a65946d117812455f9
Privacy Policy |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=d44511f91f3f1680e9212a4eaefb93b346dcdc97856c1354faf6cb5f23613cb4e413dc3fbc117bf1d5c9ad7e139764e773eb8c293fd65761
Contact the White House |

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/unsub_center.aspx?qs=d453c42a6560d1d48bd8109dd5a8e6b309d4604ed318b837fcfe4539515d3fff29b01edfc71673b9ec605ef5fe9dbb74c976a6af0c5c2013
Unsubscribe

name: Jessalyn Reid
subject: plans this weekend?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
---
"Volunteering! It's fun. It's easy. Everyone's doing it." You may have heard something like that before from folks trying to get you to volunteer for their candidate or cause. Well, guess what? They say it because it's true.

Even though I'm Tim's digital director, which means you can typically find me huddled behind a laptop, I'm out there volunteering. **In my free time (when I'm not writing emails to you) I go make calls and knock on doors for Virginia Democrats.** And you know what else? I bring my brother along, I drag my friends with me -- and they end up loving it so much they go back for more on their own.

**Are you in, too?** There are just 18 days until Election Day, when Virginians will decide our next Governor, Lieutenant Governor, Attorney General, and tons of House of Delegates seats. Right now, the Governor's race is being classified as "too close to call." **[That means that if we want to keep Virginia's leadership blue, we need every single Democrat to be a part of a major coordinated Get Out the Vote program. Click here to find an event in your neighborhood, then RSVP online.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=lfZBkeswhC93p%2FotvGeMDzJal4Dml9kD)**

When you sign up for a canvass (a.k.a. door-knocking) shift, you'll go to a meeting point at the designated time (there are three shifts tomorrow and three on Sunday), and you'll meet a bunch of other Democrats. You'll be trained on exactly where to go and how to have conversations with voters at their doors, and you'll be given all the materials you need. By the time you go to bed that night, you'll sleep well knowing you're doing every single thing you can to ensure that Virginia moves forward, not backward.

**Real talk: Virginians know better than anyone that no election is a done deal, and this year is no exception. Races can be won or lost on just a couple hundred votes. Your commitment to get out and volunteer could absolutely be the difference between those voters getting to the polls or not.**

And that's really important. I, for one, do not want to wake up on Wednesday, November 8th to Republicans having flipped Virginia and think that I could have done more. We've got to leave it all out on the field. Now's the time to close our laptops, get outside, and get out the vote for Ralph Northam, Justin Fairfax, Mark Herring, and Democrats all the way down the ticket.

**[Find an event near you and sign up for a shift this weekend. I promise, you're gonna feel great afterwards.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=5al9ovnKpX9RONXRYYuiXB9sw%2BOlvK0s)**

Talk to you soon.

-jess

Jessalyn Reid, Team Kaine Digital Director

p.s. If your schedule is jam-packed and you can't make time to volunteer, you can still support Virginia Democrats -- **[click here to make a quick online donation.](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=Zj4r1mCVVeX%2Bo5DOwjK1Xh9sw%2BOlvK0s)** | | [**DONATE**](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=2Fm%2F4CHUil9pKuX9xo0iSB9sw%2BOlvK0s)
---
| Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
---

If you no longer wish to receive updates from our campaign, please [unsubscribe](http://salsa3.salsalabs.com/dia/track.jsp?v=2&c=grOZmEJwedhuxfuUuDEr9h9sw%2BOlvK0s).
---

I wanted to make sure that you saw that Democrats have dropped their LG candidate, Justin Fairfax, from their literature with less than three weeks until Election Day (November 7th).
Why would they do that? Because Justin Fairfax is too far to the left for the Democratic ticket and his positions have cost them key support around Virginia. name: Andrew Gillum
subject: We must do better.
body:
#
Team --
We are a few short weeks away from November 9, 2017 -- one year since #45 was elected President of the United States.
We can't change the past, but we can fight for our future. I know you're tired -- and I'm tired too. But we cannot allow this constant assault on our rights and our progress to continue. If we don't work together now, and give this next cycle everything we've got, then we'll end up in the same boat after the 2018 elections.
This isn't just about our election for Florida Governor. It's about every single race up and down the ballot. And it's about the issues that will outlast this election: enshrining affordable healthcare as a right for all and lowering costs for Floridians; fighting for our public school students and teachers; and finally putting working people back at the center of our economy.
So that's why I'm making a few asks of you today.
1. **[I'm asking you to join our campaign by making a contribution of whatever is meaningful to you.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3297/9e00690/4fef949d/24031682/480692539/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-oct2017?refcode=em171020-oneyear")**
2. Then I'd like you to match that contribution to another critical campaign -- whether that be your state house, state senate, county commission, or any other local campaign.
3. Finally, please check your voter registration and make sure it's updated. You can do so [**here**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3297/9e00690/4fef949d/24031685/480692539/VEsE/ "http://dos.myflorida.com/elections/for-voters/check-your-voter-status-and-polling-place/") in Florida. Mark every single election date on your calendar, and commit to vote -- no matter how small and insignificant the race may be, I promise you, it makes a difference.

Times are tough right now across the nation, and I know many of us are experiencing a tremendous amount of stress, anger, and anxiety. But if we can find a way to channel these frustrations into action, then I promise you we will never have another day like November 9, 2016.
This is our chance to create change, you and me together along with thousands of other like-minded folks across the state and the nation, who just want a better world for everyone.
[**So I need you. I need you to fight and win, and I need you to help me bring it home for the state of Florida.**](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3297/9e00690/4fef949d/24031682/480692539/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-oct2017?refcode=em171020-oneyear")
Forward,
Andrew
**[CONTRIBUTE](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3297/9e00690/4fef949d/24031682/480692539/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/ag-oct2017?refcode=em171020-oneyear")**

Paid by Andrew Gillum, Democrat, for Governor

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3297/9e00690/4fef949d/24031684/480692539/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IiJ9/ "http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/s/receive-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip="). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3297/9e00690/4fef949d/24031687/480692539/VEsA/ "https://gillum.cp.bsd.net/page/unsubscribe/"). [You can support our campaign by making a donation.](http://act.andrewgillum.com/page/m/fcb3297/9e00690/4fef949d/24031686/480692539/VEsB/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ag-footer")

| **Numbers Tell the Story**
* * *
The story of Indiana's continued success added two new paragraphs this week. As you'll see below, yet again, our business tax climate has been ranked best in the Midwest and among the top ten states in the nation. We continue to outperform our neighboring states because of the proven Republican leadership that delivers results and progress that we elect year after year here in the Hoosier State.

And Hoosiers are taking notice. _Howey Politics Indiana_ reported this week that, "Governor **Eric Holcomb** 's job approval stands at 62% in polling...while disapproval stands at 21%." They went on to write that, "Holcomb stands at 81% among Republicans."

Both of these statistics - the latest rankings on our business climate and the Governor's approval rating - are proof positive that our plans work and our ideas are better. The numbers tell the story.

\- Kyle Hupfer

(Photos: Top - Chairman Hupfer speaking at Monday evening's Allen County Reagan Bean Dinner; Bottom - Calling numbers and serving food with Chairman Shannon Mattix at the White County GOP Bingo Night Thursday in Reynolds.)
* * *
---
| |
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
|
---
| Governor Holcomb joined local officials and the leadership of Bastian Solutions, a manufacturing company, in announcing that the company will invest $8 million in a [new center in Westfield](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=eb5ada1a2f&e=1acd9bb3d6). The facility is expected to bring in nearly 100 new jobs. Similarly, Ingram Micro Inc., a tech distribution company, [announced this week](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=803ba57418&e=1acd9bb3d6) that they would be creating up to 550 new jobs at their new facility expected to open in 2018.

In other big economic development news, Governor Holcomb announced on Wednesday that he will be taking the Hoosier Story [overseas to India](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=392e4fc350&e=1acd9bb3d6) later this month on a trade mission. Indiana is already home to multiple India-based companies and together we share a number of economic, educational and cultural ties.
---
|
---
|
---
**_Indiana's First Lady a Jack Of All Trades_** In a [Statehouse File piece](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=2b4869c6f5&e=1acd9bb3d6) released this week, First Lady **Janet Holcomb** was described as a faithful friend, exceptional cook, creative artist, and a determined firearms instructor. A graduate of Ball State University, she has always balanced her passion for creativity with her commitment to small businesses.

Although her day-to-day life changed with her husband's election as governor, friends of the First Lady still describe her as the [same genuine woman](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=c22e827252&e=1acd9bb3d6). One said: "She understands how to be a first lady she is such a perfect representative for the state, but also a perfect friend.”

[Read the full story here.](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5368160f8e&e=1acd9bb3d6)
|
---
|
---
| _**Who's Got the Best Business Tax Climate? Indiana!**_ Indiana is quickly cementing its status as the state of consistency. Yet again, we've been [ranked as having the top business tax climate in the Midwest](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=6de86584a3&e=1acd9bb3d6) \- and frankly, the competition wasn't even close. Surrounding states like Illinois, Kentucky, Wisconsin and Ohio came in at #29, #33, #38 and #45, respectively, in the nation. The Midwest states closest to us in the national rankings were Michigan, #12, and Missouri, which landed at #16. Another win for Indiana!

**Congressman Todd Rokita**[ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9d294a9fce&e=1acd9bb3d6)[rec](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=1df27b9ee9&e=1acd9bb3d6)[eives](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=99cc861515&e=1acd9bb3d6)[ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=f117575eb0&e=1acd9bb3d6)[Plainfield Chamber of Commerce Award](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=ea885fa487&e=1acd9bb3d6)

clean_body: | | | | | | | | | Numbers Tell the Story The story of Indiana's continued success added two new paragraphs this week. As you'll see below, yet again, our business tax climate has been ranked best in the Midwest and among the top ten states in the nation. We continue to outperform our neighboring states because of the proven Republican leadership that delivers results and progress that we elect year after year here in the Hoosier State. And Hoosiers are taking notice. Howey Politics Indiana reported this week that, "Governor Eric Holcomb 's job approval stands at 62% in polling...while disapproval stands at 21%." They went on to write that, "Holcomb stands at 81% among Republicans." Both of these statistics - the latest rankings on our business climate and the Governor's approval rating - are proof positive that our plans work and our ideas are better. The numbers tell the story. \- Kyle Hupfer (Photos: Top - Chairman Hupfer speaking at Monday evening's Allen County Reagan Bean Dinner; Bottom - Calling numbers and serving food with Chairman Shannon Mattix at the White County GOP Bingo Night Thursday in Reynolds.) | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | | Governor Holcomb joined local officials and the leadership of Bastian Solutions, a manufacturing company, in announcing that the company will invest $8 million in a . The facility is expected to bring in nearly 100 new jobs. Similarly, Ingram Micro Inc., a tech distribution company, that they would be creating up to 550 new jobs at their new facility expected to open in 2018. In other big economic development news, Governor Holcomb announced on Wednesday that he will be taking the Hoosier Story later this month on a trade mission. Indiana is already home to multiple India-based companies and together we share a number of economic, educational and cultural ties. | | Indiana's First Lady a Jack Of All Trades In a released this week, First Lady Janet Holcomb was described as a faithful friend, exceptional cook, creative artist, and a determined firearms instructor. A graduate of Ball State University, she has always balanced her passion for creativity with her commitment to small businesses. Although her day-to-day life changed with her husband's election as governor, friends of the First Lady still describe her as the . One said: "She understands how to be a first lady she is such a perfect representative for the state, but also a perfect friend.” | | | Who's Got the Best Business Tax Climate? Indiana! Indiana is quickly cementing its status as the state of consistency. Yet again, we've been \- and frankly, the competition wasn't even close. Surrounding states like Illinois, Kentucky, Wisconsin and Ohio came in at #29, #33, #38 and #45, respectively, in the nation. The Midwest states closest to us in the national rankings were Michigan, #12, and Missouri, which landed at #16. Another win for Indiana! | | News You Can Use Lt. Friend, For the past 272 days, the Senate, and the Republicans in the Senate have done absolutely nothing.

Friend,

For the past 272 days, the Senate, and the Republicans in the Senate have done absolutely nothing.

It's a disgrace.

For the first time in a decade, Republicans control the House, the Senate and the White House, and yet they have failed to act.

The most frustrating aspect of this failure is Obamacare. 272 days in, Obamacare is still the law of the land...

So today I’ve started a clock to keep track of the days since the Senate has acted. Every day that goes by and they fail to repeal Obamacare, the clock will keep on ticking.

If you’re tired of the failures of D.C. and Republicans in the Senate, then I’m asking you to add your name to our effort now: **[https://leahvukmir.com/will-congress-repeal-obamacare/](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=da1e420c0df64424ad36e617137cd004&_e=1zZJotqPOKEXykgtYUNYavqVCCnm9Xbjenv9dusBE8G6LTKX_gqadskjDgBYmtQT5XQllQ644IH_np230_iGrksxxQAoOafQScUGxsre2lJ6-f1VkZp-wZF1VTJ8EmaeZhtHBfrejPduXiNA_RnxyoumRQ5QZMk-23Cdjv6heVUQW-8l3gU_5Iv4LjQH3XyTlKLIhsrm_JLJTKI9hmOJ0FfCVvGT7pq_fPW8b2WHk9e33024pR3_z_WZ5sGJeaiJlRKPvlxWJE3JuaxpjtJ-L_yvUqu7Q8RLHFPPwuQNeZm8RN12A5afAuEtGx1YsBTkHlYKIH389QIAZeWSWs9IO8yvv53gywSeKrAI0HwjrC5xxnaNJZIxpaxts_OBIFum03Til5L6swcYpbovqtLTlbkihLuTeRspetT4ld2Vxh2smBFTufJuSiCABbATFE-1)**

I got into politics because I dared to question the reading practices being used in my daughter’s kindergarten class. For that, they literally sent me to the principal’s office. Can you imagine that? That moment got me started on my mission to expand opportunities for education across Wisconsin, but we have a whole lot further to go. What are the [issues](http://links.iterable.com/u/click?_t=80d255427d5b4d6fa3cb87d23cf0003e&_m=da1e420c0df64424ad36e617137cd004&_e=1zZJotqPOKEXykgtYUNYar3ExhRMKjbPje8HHOAbFiQ-bx6PTHZsO7FQSTVcBc6axx4b7tykVTAlResRraMTA_3h3nVrUpGsEwXX8k-jLr1itI3ycB7g83PrTbulwTtiKrcX6Wg6jlkP9h1cqvawUlwP36HdSI61hic9unfpF_SApsqND7zyWGJQEA-4Pi6L0NZgHm3PxOys_Utu8bTpwdFY3Iumx1ocZTuEP5iDB3KDj7Xy6JUDEyp-a76HWchjftoa6IWc3jN_rdqPKXzf4nt0mbUhkMwaraenDD-0zZpAei_x5vnQwIHXBo0_-kmvC6fTiksAcR48QMBpm1n4s_J-f6T4c4uY76_EWQyOtZr3LeK9DCbf3vO_Vvffz6ar) that you care about?
